market_data_input.key_trends: Secteur leader: energy (+23.2% YTD) | Région leader: south-korea (+54.4% YTD) | 1 secteur(s) en refroidissement (3M ≤ -2.0%) | 52W leader: information-technology (+64.6% sur 12 mois) | 6M leader: energy (+27.1% sur 6 mois)
market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: financials, communication-services, healthcare | Régions en difficulté: india | Concentration: peu de secteurs en zone favorable (rotation possible)
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.25,d=-0.04%), energy(favored,b=0.15,d=0.42%), industrials(neutral,b=0.92,d=-1.22%), materials(neutral,b=0.75,d=-0.84%), financials(neutral,b=0.96,d=-0.15%), consumer-discretionary(neutral,b=1.24,d=-0.04%), communication-services(neutral,b=1.24,d=0.55%), healthcare(neutral,b=0.6,d=-0.83%), real-estate(neutral,b=0.44,d=-1.74%), information-technology(neutral,b=1.44,d=0.07%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Brent near $100 drives persistent inflation (PCE 2.8% well above target, breakevens 2.61%) while Fed is on hold with no cuts in 6 months despite slowing momentum — classic stagflationary mix. VIX at 22 and IG/HY spreads moderately elevated signal stress building but not yet crisis; RADAR shows narrow leadership (energy only) confirming weak breadth typical of stagflation.

adjustments.action: Increase energy/oil cap — stagflation beneficiary, sector leader +23% YTD
adjustments.rationale: Brent ~$100 + energy leading all timeframes + stagflation regime = energy is the natural hedge and alpha source.

adjustments.action: Reduce AI/semi exposure — high beta growth underperforms in stagflation
adjustments.rationale: PCE 2.8% + Fed on hold = no rate relief for long-duration growth; IT sector neutral despite 64.6% 52W — momentum fading as real rates stay elevated.

adjustments.action: Increase precious metals cap — gold/silver as inflation + uncertainty hedge
adjustments.rationale: Gold at $4700 despite 11.7% drawdown from ATH signals strong structural bid; silver $75+ confirms real asset demand in inflationary regime with weakening dollar (DTWEXBGS 118 = slightly weak).

adjustments.action: Increase dividend/value tilt — defensive quality outperforms in stagflation
adjustments.rationale: Utilities favored by RADAR, consumer staples neutral with low beta — value/dividend provides income cushion when growth stalls and inflation erodes real returns.
Stagflation + weak dollar + geopolitical oil premium = gold is the primary portfolio hedge; 11.7% drawdown offers better entry than ATH.

adjustments.action: Prefer TIPS, shorten duration, avoid HY — stagflation bond playbook
adjustments.rationale: PCE 2.8% + breakevens 2.61% = real inflation risk underpriced; short duration protects against potential forced hikes if oil pushes CPI higher; HY spreads 286bps too tight for stagflation risk.

adjustments.action: Raise bond floor for Stable and Modéré — increase defensive allocation
adjustments.rationale: Equity breadth narrowing (few sectors favored, financials/healthcare struggling) warrants higher bond allocation in conservative profiles as recession risk builds behind stagflation.

adjustments.action: Raise tactical cash — VIX 22 with stagflation = elevated tail risk, preserve optionality
adjustments.rationale: VIX 22 + narrow market leadership + oil shock risk = maintain dry powder; T-bills at ~3.6-4% yield make cash productive while waiting for potential dislocation.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Stagflation playbook: TIPS for inflation protection, short duration to limit rate risk if Fed forced to hike, avoid HY credit where spreads don't compensate for rising default risk.

cash_allocation.rationale: VIX 22 + stagflation uncertainty + narrow equity leadership = cash as a call option on future dislocations; T-bills yielding ~4% make this productive, not punitive.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Crypto is a risk asset that underperforms in stagflation; reduce caps but maintain small Agressif allocation as digital gold narrative has some validity with weak dollar.

warnings: OIL SHOCK ESCALATION: Brent at $99 — breach of $105-110 could trigger CPI spike forcing emergency Fed hike, crushing both bonds and equities simultaneously
FED POLICY ERROR: Fed on hold at 3.64% with PCE 2.8% — if inflation reaccelerates, forced tightening into slowing economy = hard landing scenario
HY SPREAD COMPLACENCY: HY OAS at 286bps is historically tight for a stagflationary environment — potential violent repricing if defaults tick up or liquidity tightens
GOLD DRAWDOWN TRAP: 11.7% drawdown from ATH could deepen if Fed turns hawkish; however structural bid from central banks and weak dollar should provide floor
EQUITY BREADTH COLLAPSE: Only energy in 'favored' zone, 3 sectors in difficulty — classic late-cycle narrowing that often precedes broader correction
DOLLAR WEAKNESS ACCELERATION: DTWEXBGS at 118 (slightly weak) + EUR/USD 1.17 — further dollar decline amplifies imported inflation and commodity price pressure

market_interpretation.geopolitical_risk: Brent near $100 implies significant geopolitical/supply premium — likely Middle East tensions or OPEC+ discipline creating energy shock conditions that feed through to global inflation.

adjustments_output.cash_tactical_rationale: VIX 22 + stagflation uncertainty + narrow equity leadership = cash as a call option on future dislocations; T-bills yielding ~4% make this productive, not punitive.
